mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting central bank intervention and positive SPX returns through risk-on sentiment in the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in triggered periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and disinflation (INDPRO up, CPIAUCSL down) signals strong real economic activity with stable pricing, driving positive SPX returns over 20 trading days via improved earnings outlook.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-moving INDPRO/CPIAUCSL regimes are negative or interaction effect size < 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and rising) combined with core inflation above 2% creates a 'stagflation' risk, leading to negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in triggered regimes exceed -5% or interaction effect size > 0.20 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates robust labor market demand, driving positive SPX returns over 5 trading days through corporate revenue optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high PAYEMS periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST < 650k) signals weak consumer demand, generating negative SPX returns over 20 trading days through reduced corporate investment expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low HOUST regimes exceed -3% or single-series effect size < 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive SPX returns over 60 trading days via sustained corporate earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-moving GDPC1/RSAFS regimes are negative or interaction effect size < 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 94.5) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative SPX returns over 5 trading days through deflationary risk and earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low INDPRO periods exceed -2% or single-series effect size < 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.
